idCitation.resTitle: United States Court of Appeals Circuits

dataIdInfo.idPurp: This data provides the boundaries of the United States Court of Appeals.
dataIdInfo.idAbs: Created based upon information located on the US Department of Justice website (https://www.fjc.gov/history/courts/u.s.-courts-appeals-and-federal-judiciary) 2021.

Consts.useLimit:
Access Constraint: None (Public Domain Information)

Use Constraint: None (Public Use)

themeKeys.keyword: United States, US, law enforcement, federal district courts, jurisdictions

placeKeys.keyword: District of Columbia, Continental United States, Northern Mariana Islands, Guam, Alaska, Hawaii, U.S. Virgin Islands, Puerto Rico, CONUS

dataIdInfo.suppInfo: While appeals for the 9th Circuit are heard in the James R. Browning Federal Courthouse in San Francisco, California; they are also heard in the Richard H. Chambers Courthouse in Pasadena, California, the Pioneer Courthouse in Portland, Oregon, and the William K. Nakamura Courthouse in Seattle, Washington, due to the 9th Circuit being the largest in the United States. Appeal cases for Puerto Rico (First Circuit) are heard in the John Joseph Moakley Courthouse in Boston, Massachusetts. There is another circuit courthouse located in the Jose V. Toledo Federal Building and U.S. Courthouse, San Juan, Puerto Rico, where the court sits for two weeks a year.

enttyp.enttypd: The boundaries of the twelve circuits of the United States Court of Appeals (including Washington, DC).

attrdomv.udom: Sequential unique whole numbers that are automatically generated.

edom.edomvd: The court has appellate jurisdiction over the United States district courts for Maine, Massachusetts, New Hamphshire, and Puerto Rico.
The court has appellate jurisdiction over the United States district courts for Connecticut, New York, and Vermont.
The court has appellate jurisdiction over the United States district courts for Delaware, New Jersey, Pennsylvania, and US Virgin Islands.
The court has appellate jurisdiction over the United States district courts for Maryland, North Carolina, South Carolina, Virginia, and West Virginia.
The court has appellate jurisdiction over the United States district courts for Louisiana, Mississippi, and Texas.
The court has appellate jurisdiction over the United States district courts for Kentucky, Michigan, Ohio, and Tennessee.
The court has appellate jurisdiction over the United States district courts for Illinois, Indiana, and Wisconsin.
The court has appellate jurisdiction over the United States district courts for Arkansas, Iowa, Minnesota, Missouri, Nebraska, North Dakota, and South Dakota.
The court has appellate jurisdiction over the Unnited States district courts for Alaska, Arizona, California, Guam, Hawaii, Idaho, Montana, Nevada, Northern Mariana Islands, Oregon, and Washington.
The court has appellate jurisdiction over the United States district courts for Colorado, Kansas, New Mexico, Oklahoma, Utah, and Wyoming.
The court has appellate jurisdiction over the United States district courts for Alabama, Florida, and Georgia.
The court has appellate jurisdiction over the United States district courts for the District of Columbia.

codesetd.codesetn: States and Equivalent Areas within the United States, Puerto Rico, and the Insular Areas

attrdomv.udom: This field contains text values that are unrepresentable as other domain types.

attr.attrdef: The name of the current chief judge.

attrdomv.udom: This field contains text values that are unrepresentable as other domain types.

attr.attrdef: The U.S. president that nominated the judge to the court.

attrdomv.udom: This field contains numeric values that are unrepresentable as other domain types.

attr.attrdef: The year the judge began their term as a chief judge.
Length of feature in internal units.

attrdomv.udom: Positive real numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: The city in which the courthouse is located.

attrdomv.udom: This field contains text values that are unrepresentable as other domain types.

attr.attrdef: Area of feature in internal units squared.

attrdomv.udom: Positive real numbers that are automatically generated.

attr.attrdef: The name of the courthouse or building which the court of appeals is located.

attrdomv.udom: This field contains text values that are unrepresentable as other domain types.

attr.attrdef: The address of the courthouse or building where the court of appeals presides.

attrdomv.udom: This field contains text values that are unrepresentable as other domain types.

citeinfo.title: United States Court of Appeals Circuits

descript.abstract: Created based upon information located on the US Department of Justice website (https://www.fjc.gov/history/courts/u.s.-courts-appeals-and-federal-judiciary), and the United States Courts website (https://www.uscourts.gov/federal-court-finder/), 2021.
descript.purpose: This data provides the boundaries of the United States Court of Appeals.
descript.supplinf: While appeals for the 9th Circuit are heard in the James R. Browning Federal Courthouse in San Francisco, California; they are also heard in the Richard H. Chambers Courthouse in Pasadena, California, the Pioneer Courthouse in Portland, Oregon, and the William K. Nakamura Courthouse in Seattle, Washington, due to the 9th Circuit being the largest in the United States. Appeal cases for Puerto Rico (First Circuit) are heard in the John Joseph Moakley Courthouse in Boston, Massachusetts. There is another circuit courthouse located in the Jose V. Toledo Federal Building and U.S. Courthouse, San Juan, Puerto Rico, where the court sits for two weeks a year. Appeals cases for the Eighth Circuit are primarily heard in the Thomas F. Eagleton United States Courthouse in St. Louis, Missouri and secondarily at the Warren E. Burger United States Courthouse in St. Paul, Minnesota. Appeals cases for the Eleventh Circuit are primarily heard at the Elbert P. Tuttle Court of Appeals Building in Atlanta, Georgia as "All Non-ECF Filings and correspondence should be sent to the Atlanta location." per the United States Court website (https://www.ca11.uscourts.gov/court-info/court-locations/atlanta-georgia), however the Eleventh Circuit hears appeals cases at the James Lawrence King Federal Justice Building, in Miami, Florida.

vertacc.vertaccr: None; dataset contains no vertical data.

procstep.procdesc: Dataset created by using US Census Bureau's 2018 County Boundary data found on the website - https://www.census.gov/geographies/mapping-files/time-series/geo/carto-boundary-file.html The shapefile was converted into a file geodatabase and reprojected to WGS1984 format. The base county polygons were merged to create jurisdictional boundary polygons based on information gathered from the US Department of Justice website - https://www.fjc.gov/history/courts/u.s.-courts-appeals-and-federal-judiciary

attracc.attraccr: No formal attribute accuracy test was conducted. Data from the authoritative sources (as identified in other sections of this metadata) is assumed to be correct and accurate. See also “Overview Description” in the “Entities and Attributes” metadata section.

dataqual.logic: Data were extracted from sources and other open source websites to populate the attribute fields; NOT AVAILABLE values (for string and date data types) and Null values (for numeric data types) denote missing data that was not present in the source data. During quality control, attributes were checked to ensure that they fell within the expected ranges or had expected/valid values. Text attribute values were checked for spelling and usage consistency. No topology tests were conducted. All records were verified to occur within the spatial domain of the feature class.
dataqual.complete: Data is complete as of the most recent date indicated in the Time Period section.

stdorder.fees: None. No fees are applicable for obtaining the data set.

distinfo.distliab: Distributor assumes no liability for misuse of data.

metainfo.metstdn: FGDC Content Standards for Digital Geospatial Metadata
